chapter_title.en: Chapter on a Man Who Bequeaths a Portion of His Wealth to Another and is Then Killed by Mistake

text: 5537 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِثُلُثِ مَالِهِ ثُمَّ قُتِلَ خَطَأً قَالَ "ثُلُثُ دِيَتِهِ دَاخِلٌ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5537 - In another narration, Abu Abdullah (as), was asked about a man who had bequeathed one-third of his wealth and was then killed by mistake.
Imam (as) said: "One-third of his blood money (diyyah) is included in his bequest."
